title: Deliciously Dehydrated Middle Eastern Eggs Shakshuka
description: A herbal and velvety Middle Eastern dish featuring chilli, this kosher-friendly shakshuka is a crowd-pleaser. It combines the flavors of dehydrated ingredients with fresh eggs and spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large oven-safe skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced red bell pepper and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the rehydrated dehydrated tomatoes, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and chilli flakes.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Create 4 wells in the tomato mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, until the eggs are cooked to desired doneness.
Remove from the oven and serve hot.
